title: Nessi User Journey
description: Structured journey data for the Nessi docs site. Source of truth for flow visualizations, coverage tracking, and test planning.

domain.description: Feature domain this journey belongs to. Used for grouping in the docs UI.

title.description: Short, clean journey name (max 30 chars). Use plain language describing the feature or user goal. No parenthetical tech details, arrows, implementation notes, or file references. Good: 'Account Deletion', 'Shipping Addresses', 'Cart & Checkout'. Bad: 'Account Deletion (Full Cascade)', 'Guest Cart → Login → Merge'.

persona.description: Which persona this journey belongs to

description.description: One-line summary of what this journey covers

relatedIssues.description: GitHub issue numbers that built this flow

flows.description: Ordered list of flows within this journey.

title.description: Flow title shown in the UI

trigger.description: What initiates this flow (e.g., 'User clicks Sign Up')

id.description: Step identifier, referenceable from conductor tickets

title.description: Short, human-readable title for the node (max 24 chars). Use plain language describing what happens from the user's perspective. Examples: 'Save Address', 'Validate Cart', 'Send Welcome Email'. Avoid: arrows (→), em-dashes (—), HTTP methods, camelCase, technical jargon, file paths, or code references.

label.description: DEPRECATED — use 'title' instead. Kept for backwards compatibility during migration.

action.description: For client-layer steps: the user interaction that triggers this step. Shown as a small badge on the node.

method.description: For server-layer steps: the HTTP method. Shown as a pill badge on the node. Do NOT include the method in the title — it's displayed separately.

layer.description: Where this step executes. 'client' = visible in browser. 'server' = API route / server action. 'database' = trigger or RLS. 'background' = silent client-side (merge, sync). 'email' = transactional email. 'external' = third-party (Stripe, etc.).

route.description: API route or page route (e.g., '/api/addresses', '/invite/[token]'). Do NOT include the HTTP method here — use the 'method' field instead.

tooltip.description: Rich explanation shown when hovering the node. Use this for implementation details, context, or any information that won't fit in the title. Supports longer text (up to 300 chars).

codeRef.description: Path to the primary source file (e.g., 'src/features/auth/services/auth.ts')

notes.description: Internal implementation notes or testing details (shown in tooltip)

errorCases.description: Known error/edge cases at this step

branches.description: Decision points that split the flow into paths

afterStep.description: Step ID where the branch occurs

condition.description: Short yes/no question (e.g., 'Has addresses?', 'Authenticated?'). Keep under 30 chars.

label.description: Short branch label: 'Yes', 'No', 'Expired', etc. Keep to 1-2 words. Any additional context (like 'empty state shown' or 'button disabled') goes in 'detail'.

detail.description: Optional context shown in a tooltip when hovering this branch option. Use for state descriptions, UI behavior, or edge case explanations. Example: 'Empty state: Add your first address'

goTo.description: Step ID to continue at, or 'END' for terminal

connections.description: Explicit step-to-step connections (when not simply sequential)

labelGuidelines.description: Guidelines for writing good step titles (for documentation, not validation)

client.title: Open Address Form
client.tooltip: AddressFormModal opens with empty fields for label, address lines, city, state, zip, and default toggle

client.title: Enter Address Details
client.tooltip: Fields: label, line1, line2, city, state, zip, is_default

client.title: Confirm Deletion
client.tooltip: Confirmation dialog with 'Are you sure?' prompt

server.title: Save Address
server.tooltip: Validates address fields, checks 5-address cap, creates record with optional default promotion

server.title: Update Default
server.tooltip: Sets this address as default, clears default flag from previous default

server.title: Fetch Cart Items
server.tooltip: Returns cart items joined with listing data, grouped by seller. Includes price-change detection.

database.title: Cascade Delete Members
database.tooltip: CASCADE: members row deleted triggers handle_member_deletion() — releases member slug + owned shop slugs

rules: Titles must be ≤ 24 characters — short, clear, action-oriented
Use plain English verbs: Save, Fetch, Update, Delete, Open, Show, Send, Check
Never include HTTP methods in titles — use the 'method' field
Never include route paths in titles — use the 'route' field
Never use arrows (→) or em-dashes (—) in titles
Never use camelCase or PascalCase in titles
Never include parenthetical details in titles — use 'tooltip' field
For client steps, include 'action' to show user interaction type
For server steps, include 'method' and 'route' as separate fields
Branch labels should be 1-2 words max (Yes, No, Expired, etc.)
Branch detail goes in 'detail' field, not in the label
Use 'tooltip' for anything that won't fit in 24 chars
